summary: Matched the Parasol item module by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itparasol.s with src/melee/it/items/itparasol.c and wiring the C object into obj_files.mk. The new C file reconstructs the item state table, state-transition callbacks, physics/deceleration behavior, collision callbacks, animation-speed wrappers, and a JObj rotation update path. One difficult function, it_8028B1A4, keeps a MUST_MATCH inline-assembly version while also providing a readable C version outside MUST_MATCH. The PR also added itparasol.h prototypes, exposed itColl_BounceOffVictim in it_266F.h, and added HSD_JObjGetRotationY to jobj.h to support the decompiled code.
what_changed: Added a 421-line C implementation at src/melee/it/items/itparasol.c.
Updated obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/it/items/itparasol.c.o instead of the old asm object.
Added an itparasol.h header with prototypes for the it_8028B08C through it_8028B8B8 Parasol functions.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F5AB0 in C, with state entries that dispatch to callbacks such as it_8028B144, it_8028B1A4, it_8028B2EC, it_8028B42C, it_8028B5B4, it_8028B60C, and it_8028B614.

Implemented item behavior around GET_ITEM, Item_80268E5C state changes, it_8026B390 reset logic, fall-speed updates through it_80272860, x40_vel deceleration, bounce-off-victim/shield collision callbacks, and animation frame speed updates.
Added a prototype for bool itColl_BounceOffVictim(Item_GObj*) in src/melee/it/it_266F.h, mirroring the existing itColl_BounceOffShield declaration.
Added static inline f32 HSD_JObjGetRotationY(HSD_JObj* jobj) to src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h with HSD_ASSERT(730, jobj) and return jobj->rotate.y.
smart_moves: Used small local inline helpers, including fabsf, jobj_child, decelerateItemX, and animSpeed, to express repeated assembly patterns without spreading duplicated control flow across many callbacks.

Kept the hard-to-match JObj rotation callback it_8028B1A4 under #ifdef MUST_MATCH as inline assembly while also providing a readable C implementation for non-MUST_MATCH builds. This preserved matching pressure without losing maintainability.

Recreated HSD assertion strings for the MUST_MATCH assembly path, including "jobj.h", "jobj", and "!(jobj->flags & JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION)", so the inlined baselib-style checks could match original codegen/data references.
Moved a generally useful JObj accessor, HSD_JObjGetRotationY, into jobj.h rather than open-coding all rotation-y access in the item module.

Factored nearly identical animation state entry functions through animSpeed(item_gobj, speed, statenum), covering it_8028B618, it_8028B648, it_8028B6B0, it_8028B718, it_8028B780, it_8028B7E8, and it_8028B850.
decomp_lessons: For item modules, matching often means reconstructing both the state table and many small callback wrappers, not just the visibly complex physics functions.
When an HSD_JObj inline setter/getter sequence is involved, adding a precise baselib inline helper can be preferable to forcing item-local pointer field accesses.
MUST_MATCH guarded inline assembly can be a pragmatic bridge for one stubborn function while the rest of an item file moves from asm to C.
Small hand-written math helpers like fabsf may be needed to reproduce original float compare/fneg control flow instead of relying on library calls or compiler builtins.
Repeated item state wrappers should be factored carefully only when the helper shape still preserves the original call order, constants, and generated code.
Header work is part of matching: exposing itColl_BounceOffVictim and declaring item-local functions allowed the new C file to replace the assembly object cleanly.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions kept address-based names such as it_8028B08C, it_8028B1A4, it_8028B5B4, and it_8028B8B8.
Collision helpers use the existing item collision naming pattern itColl_BounceOffShield and itColl_BounceOffVictim.
The new item header follows the module name itparasol.h and include guard MELEE_IT_ITPARASOL_H.
Item state table/static data retained address-style symbols such as it_803F5AB0 and it_803F5B60.
Local helpers use descriptive lowercase names where they are not original symbols, including jobj_child, decelerateItemX, animSpeed, and fabsf.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the old assembly file only after substituting a C object in obj_files.mk.
Used #ifdef MUST_MATCH around asm bool it_8028B1A4(Item_GObj* item_gobj), including nofralloc and explicit labels, for exact matching of the JObj rotation update path.

Provided a non-MUST_MATCH C version of it_8028B1A4 that reads item special attributes, gets the child JObj, reads rotation Y, adds a deg_to_rad scaled velocity/facing adjustment, and writes the rotation back with HSD_JObjSetRotationY.
Encoded the old small-data/assert strings used by the inline assembly path as static char arrays.
Used inline helpers to shape codegen for absolute value, null-child JObj access, X velocity deceleration, and repeated animation-state transitions.
Preserved item transition constants and flags such as ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, ITEM_DROP_UPDATE, state IDs 0 through 10, and Item_8026AE84 arguments 0xF7, 0x7F, 0x40.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR context slice, so no human review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Can the MUST_MATCH inline assembly version of it_8028B1A4 be eliminated after improving HSD_JObj inline/assert matching?
Should the itparasol.h extern ItemStateTable declaration be audited against the C symbol name it_803F5AB0, since the slice shows an extern named it_803F70B8?
Are there other item modules that can reuse HSD_JObjGetRotationY or the same JObj rotation-y matching pattern?
Can the local fabsf or decelerateItemX patterns be standardized if repeated in nearby item decompilations?
